attributes.retriever.query: What are the SLA guarantees for the enterprise tier?

retriever.documents.title: Enterprise SLA Agreement v3.2
retriever.documents.content: Enterprise tier customers receive a 99.95% uptime SLA with automated credits. Incident response times: P1 critical within 15 minutes, P2 high within 1 hour, P3 medium within 4 hours. Monthly uptime is calculated excluding scheduled maintenance windows announced 72 hours in advance.

retriever.documents.title: Enterprise SLA Agreement v3.2
retriever.documents.content: SLA credit schedule: Below 99.95% but above 99.9% — 10% credit. Below 99.9% but above 99.0% — 25% credit. Below 99.0% — 50% credit. Credits are applied to the next billing cycle and do not exceed the monthly subscription fee.

retriever.documents.title: Pricing and Plans Overview
retriever.documents.content: Enterprise plans include dedicated infrastructure, custom data residency, SSO/SCIM provisioning, and a named account manager. SLA-backed uptime guarantees apply to all production endpoints.

retriever.documents.title: Support Tiers Comparison
retriever.documents.content: Enterprise support includes 24/7 coverage with phone, email, and Slack channels. Dedicated support engineer assigned within 48 hours of contract signing. Quarterly business reviews included.

retriever.documents.title: Platform Architecture Overview
retriever.documents.content: The platform is deployed across three AWS regions (us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1) with active-active replication. Failover is automatic with sub-30-second DNS propagation.

llm.prompt.content: You are a helpful assistant that answers questions using only the provided context. If the context does not contain enough information, say so. Cite document sources.

Context:
[1] Enterprise SLA Agreement v3.2 (chunk 2): Enterprise tier customers receive a 99.95% uptime SLA with automated credits. Incident response times: P1 critical within 15 minutes, P2 high within 1 hour, P3 medium within 4 hours. Monthly uptime is calculated excluding scheduled maintenance windows announced 72 hours in advance.

[2] Enterprise SLA Agreement v3.2 (chunk 3): SLA credit schedule: Below 99.95% but above 99.9% — 10% credit. Below 99.9% but above 99.0% — 25% credit. Below 99.0% — 50% credit. Credits are applied to the next billing cycle and do not exceed the monthly subscription fee.

[3] Pricing and Plans Overview (chunk 1): Enterprise plans include dedicated infrastructure, custom data residency, SSO/SCIM provisioning, and a named account manager. SLA-backed uptime guarantees apply to all production endpoints.

Question: What are the SLA guarantees for the enterprise tier?

llm.response.content:
The enterprise tier includes the following SLA guarantees:

**Uptime**: 99.95% uptime SLA with automated credits [1].

**Incident Response Times** [1]:
- P1 (Critical): 15 minutes
- P2 (High): 1 hour
- P3 (Medium): 4 hours

**SLA Credit Schedule** [2]:
- 99.9%--99.95% uptime: 10% credit
- 99.0%--99.9% uptime: 25% credit
- Below 99.0% uptime: 50% credit

Credits are applied to the next billing cycle and capped at the monthly subscription fee. Scheduled maintenance windows (announced 72 hours ahead) are excluded from uptime calculations [1].

Additionally, the SLA covers all production endpoints and includes dedicated infrastructure [3].
